data.body: EXECUTE COMPLETE (human-approved deletion). RESULT: 63,157 comments deleted (204) + 1,381 idempotent already-gone (404); log at jira-cleanup/deletions-*.jsonl. All 13 tickets dropped from 5000 (capped) to their distinct-comment counts, ALL far below the 5000 cap -> new comments can sync again: REB-155=76 REB-352=64 REB-428=31 REB-341=31 REB-289=29 REB-427=27 REB-183=25 REB-327=25 REB-424=25 REB-425=25 REB-190=22 REB-426=22 REB-453=22. SAFETY: full 222MB backup (id/body/author/created) taken for all 13 BEFORE any deletion; 0 human comments touched (all 65k were bot-marked, single accountId 712020:6471376f...); kept the oldest of each distinct normalized body. NOTE: REB-155 (76) and REB-289 (29) settled slightly above my dry-run distinct estimate (69/24) because the live reconciler (not quiesced during the ~1hr run) re-synced ~12 comments my _normalize_comment_body collapsed more aggressively than the reconciler's own outbound dedup — benign: they converged to the reconciler's steady state (== bd80's recorded local-distinct 76/29), no content lost. Steady-state '0 re-adds' is now operator-attestable against the next green reconcile-bridge run (counts match local-distinct). AC: below-cap ✓, backup ✓, dry-run+approval ✓, keep-oldest bot-only ✓, deletion-log ✓.
